title: Roettlera humboldtiana
intro: Roettlera humboldtiana, also known as Devil's Claw, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Pedaliaceae. The root of Roettlera humboldtiana has been traditionally used in folk medicine to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and anti-diarrheal properties. The bioactive constituents of Roettlera humboldtiana include iridoid glycosides, particularly harpagoside, as well as other compounds like alkaloids and flavonoids. Common herbal preparations made with Roettlera humboldtiana include root powder, capsules, and teas, which are often used to support joint health, reduce muscle pain, and promote digestive well-being.

preparations: Roettlera humboldtiana is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region. Herbal preparations from Roettlera humboldtiana are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, pain, and respiratory problems.
